title: parageum calthifolium
intro: Parageum calthifolium, also known as Marsh Marigold, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Ranunculaceae family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and shiny green leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating skin conditions such as wounds, eczema, and acne. The health benefits of Parageum calthifolium include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in tinctures, infusions, and salves, which are applied topically to the affected area to harness its medicinal properties.
